title: Lrs Of Mohammad Kamil vs Sardar Khan on 29 June, 2022
body: Lrs Of Mohammad Kamil vs Sardar Khan on 29 June, 2022 Author: Rekha Borana Bench: Rekha Borana HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE FOR RAJASTHAN AT JODHPUR S.B. Civil Revision Petition No. 80/2021 Lrs Of Mohammad Kamil, Bamba Choti Haweli Ke Pass, Jodhpur 1. Mohd. Arif S/o Mohd. Kamil, age 38 years 2. Mohd. Aameen S/o Mohd. Kamil, age 33 years 3. Tasleem Bano D/o Mohd. Kamil, age 40 years 4. Nigar Khan S/o Mohd. Kamil, age 35 years 5. Sabiya D/o Mohd. Kamil, age 31 years 6. Naseem Bano W/o Mohd. Kamil, age 56 years By caste Musalman, Residents of Near Bamba Chhoti Haveli, Jodhpur. ----Petitioners Versus Sardar Khan S/o Umrav Khan Pathan, Bamba Choti Haweli Ke Pass, Jodhpur ----Respondent AND S.B. Civil Revision Petition No. 81/2021 Lrs Of Mohammad Kamil, Bamba Chhoti Haweli Ke Pas, Johdpur ----Petitioners Versus Sardar Khan S/o Umrav Khan Pathan, Bamba Chhoti Haweli Ke Pas, Johdpur ----Respondent For Petitioner(s) : Mr. S.M. Naveen For Respondent(s) : Mr. Narendra Thanvi HON'BLE MS. JUSTICE REKHA BORANA Order 29/06/2022 The present revision petitions have been filed against the order dated 23.09.2021 passed by the Civil Judge (J.D.) No.3, Jodhpur Metropolitan, Jodhpur whereby the applications under (Downloaded on 26/12/2022 at 06:12:57 AM) (2 of 4) [CR-80/2021] Order 21 Rule 97 read with Sections 47 & 101 , CPC have been rejected at the cost of Rs.2,500/-. Before proceeding on to the merits of the present matters it is essential to note the brief facts which culminated into the present impugned orders. A civil suit was filed by plaintiff-Sardar Khan against the present petitioner-defendant for eviction and for arrears of rent. The said suit was decreed in favour of the plaintiff-Sardar Khan and first appeal against the same was also dismissed. Thereafter, a second appeal was preferred by the defendant Mohd. Kamil and on 24.04.2019, the same was admitted after framing three substantial questions of law. On the said date, an interim order was also passed in favour of the defendant subject to certain conditions. Admittedly, the said conditions were not complied with by the petitioners and an application for extension of time to comply with the conditions was moved in the said second appeal. Vide order dated 15.12.2020, the application of the petitioners was rejected and it was specifically declared/clarified that the interim order dated 24.04.2019 stands vacated. It was further observed by the appellate Court that the respondent shall be free to execute the decree in accordance with law. After the said order dated 15.12.2020 being passed, the plaintiff-respondents proceeded on to pursue the execution petitions in which an applications under Order 21 Rule 97 read with Sections 47 & 101 , CPC were preferred by the defendant- petitioner. The said applications were dismissed vide orders dated 23.09.2021 against which the present revision petitions have been filed. (Downloaded on 26/12/2022 at 06:12:57 AM) (3 of 4) [CR-80/2021] Learned counsel for the petitioners submitted that the learned Court below has erred in rejecting the objections of the petitioners in so far as the Court below failed to consider that the plaintiff had committed a fraud with the Court by concealing certain important documents which led to passing of the decree by the Court. Learned counsel further submitted that the possession of the land had been handed over by the plaintiff-Sardar Khan himself to the defendants and they are in the possession of the property till date. He also submitted that plaintiff-Sardar Khan who claims to be the owner of the property is to the contrary, his tenant and the executing Court rejected the objections of the petitioners without considering all these aspects. Per contra learned counsel for the respondent submitted that the grounds as raised by the petitioners in the present petitions in form of application under Order 21 Rule 97 read with Sections 47 & 101 , CPC are those which have already been raised and decided in the suit and affirmed in the first appeal. So far as the issues in dispute are concerned, the second appeal of the defendant is already pending adjudication before the competent authority and therefore, the same issues cannot be gone into by this Court in revisional jurisdiction. Heard learned counsel for the parties and perused the material available on record. The present are the cases wherein the objections as raised by the petitioners in the form of applications under Order 21 Rule 97 read with Sections 47 & 101 , CPC are those which have been raised in the second appeal preferred by them. Keeping into consideration the said grounds, appropriate substantial questions of law have already been framed by the appellate Court and the (Downloaded on 26/12/2022 at 06:12:57 AM) (4 of 4) [CR-80/2021] same are pending adjudication. As of the date, this findings as recorded by the First Appellate Court are in vogue and this Court, in revisional jurisdiction, cannot sit over the judgment and decree of the First Appellate Court and that too, on the issues of which the adjudication is pending before the Second Appellate Court. So far as the execution of the decrees is concerned, the same have been proceeded upon. In view of the fact that the petitioners did not comply with the conditions incorporated in the interim order granted by the appellate Court in the second appeal, the appellate Court did not interfere in the time extension application also and made it clear that the decree holder would be free to execute the decree. The said order has not been challenged by the judgment debtors and therefore, the proceedings undertaken by the executing Court are perfectly in terms of law and completely valid. There being no interim order in favour of the judgment debtors as of date, the executing Court is free to proceed with the execution proceedings in terms of law. So far as the plea of the fraud is concerned, counsel has not submitted any such fact which can be termed to be a fraud being acted upon the Court. In view of the above observations, this Court finds no ground to interfere with the impugned orders dated 23.09.2021. Resultantly, the present revisions petition are dismissed. All the pending applications also stand disposed of. Let a copy of this order be placed in each file. (REKHA BORANA),J 69,70-T.Singh, Abhishek/- (Downloaded on 26/12/2022 at 06:12:57 AM) Powered by TCPDF (www.tcpdf.org)
